r: a, fatḥa, cedar, u, fun, kasra, i, fin, ẓamma, bull., ā, father, au, ou, house, ai, aisle., rule, oo, pool, o, pole., ī, caprice, e, fête., Abjad, aśuddha, an, ananta
def: the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'
s.m. Father. (Rare.)
wety: Formed by analogy after جب (jab) and تب (tab).

ety: Old P. āw, Pehl. āp, Zend ap, S. -अप्
def: s.m. Water; water or lustre (in gems); temper (of steel, &c.); edge or sharpness (of a sword, &c.); sparkle, lustre; splendour; elegance; dignity, honour, character, reputation. — āb-āb honā, To be drenched with perspiration; to be thoroughly ashamed or abashed; to blush. — īb-utarnā, To lose lustre or polish, become dull; to rade; to lose honour, be disgraced. — āb-bigaṛnā = āb utarnā. — āb-i-bārān, s.m. Rain-water. — āb-bāzī, s.f. Play or sport in water; swimming. — āb-i-basta, s.m. Ice; glass. — āb-pāshī, s.f. Sprinkling water; watering fields, &c.; irrigation. — āb-tāb (and āb o tāb), s.f. Brightness, brilliance, lustre, splendour; dignity, grandeur, majesty, glory, pomp
subs: (-ko), To impart lustre (to); to glorify, praise, honour. — āb-tāba, s.m. An ewer with a spout, a jug. — āb-i-jārī, s.m. Running water, stream, rivulet; tears running down the cheeks. — āb jānā = āb utarnā, q.v. — āb-i-ioë, s.m. Water of a rivulet. — āb-jo, s.f. Rivulet, steamlet. — āb-josh, s.m. Juice of cooked meat, broth, soup, gravy. — āb ćaṛhānā (-par), To cause lustre to come (upon), to give brightness (to), to make bright, burnish, polish; to temper (steel, &c.); to whet, sharpen (a sword, &c.). — āb-i-ćashm, s.m. Water of the eyes, tears. — āb-ćashī, s.f. Giving an infant water to drink for the first time (generally when about six months old) preparatory to weaning. — āb-i-haram, s.m. Unclean or forbiddea water; wine; hypocritical tears. — āb-i-ḥasrat, s.m. Water of desire; wish, louging, appetite. — āb-i-ḥayāt, s.m. Water of life; immortality; fountain of life; a fabulous fountain (said to be veiled in obscurity); inspired knowledge ( = āb-i-ḵẖiẓr); clear, cold, sweet water; drinking water used by a king. — āb-i-ḥaiwān, s.m. = āb-i-haiyāt, q.v. — āb-ḵẖāna, s.m. Place where drinking water is kept, repository of water; reservoir. — āb-ḵẖāst, s.m. Melon, cucumber. — āb-i-ḵẖiẓr, s.m. Water of life, &c.; inspired knowledge (see āb-i-haiyāt, and also ḵẖiẓr). — āb-ḵẖur and āb-ḵẖurd, s.m. Drinking; meat and drink ( = āb-dāna and dānā-pānī), victuals; place of drinking water; fortune, lot, destiny. — āb-ḵẖora, s.m. A narrow-mouthed earthen or metal vessel for holding water (syn. kūza); goblet; cup of water

To offer a libation of cups of sherbet or milk. — āb-dār, s.m. The servant who has charge of the drinking water; — adj. Polished, bright; of a good water (as gems); well-tempered (as steel, &c.); sharp (as a sword. &c.); pure, clean, white

s.m. The place where drinking water is kept. — āb-dārī, s.f. The office of āb-dār; brilliancy (of gems); lustre, polish; sharpness (of a sword, &c.); purity, clearness, whiteness. — āb-dāna (and āo o dāna), s.m. Water and grain; meat and drink ( = dānā-pīnī), food, victuals; means of subsistence, livelihood, employment; lot, fortune, destiny. — āb-dast, s.m. Purificatory washing (such as washing the hands, &c. preparatory to prayer, or before eating, or after an unclean act); ablution; washing oneself after easing nature (this is the common signification of the word in India); a skilful workman (one whose hands turn out clean and polished work, — a rare signification); — adj. Having clean hands, pure, spotless (rare)
To wash or purify (the hands, &c.); to wash oneself after easing nature
Idem. — āb-i-dandān, s.m. Sharpness and polish of the teeth; weakness, baseness (rare)

s.m. Watering of the mouth; a species of pear; a species of pomegranate; a kind of delicious sweetmeat; — adj. Weak, vile, (rare). — āb-i-dahān, s.m. Water of the mouth, saliva. — āb-i-dīda, s.m. Water of the eyes, tears
adj. & adv. Having tears in the eyes, in tears
To have tears in the eyes, to shed tears. — āb-rū, s.f. Lit. 'brightness of face'; honour, character, reputation, rank, dignity; grandeur; pride, credit, ornament, show, appearance
(-kī), To drag down the character or reputation (of), to disgrace, dishonour; to abuse, vilify; to violate the chastity (of), to debauch; to commit an indecent assault (upon)
(-kī), To give one's honour or character to the winds, to sacrifice one's reputation; to destroy the reputation or character (of)
q.v. — āb-rū pānā, To obtain rank, dignity, or renown
(-kī), To lay one's honour in the dust; to destroy the character or reputation (of)
To pay or do honour (to); to confer rank or dignity (on), to dignify, ennoble, exalt; to sacrifice or lose one's own honour or character
To preserve one's honour or reputation
To be bent on ruining one's honour, character, or reputation
(-kī), To pay or do honour (to), to treat with respect
(-kī) = āb-rū-kā lāgū honā, q.v. — āb-rū lenā (kī) = āb-rū utārnā, q.v.
To be stained in character or reputation

Idem. — āb-i-ravān, s.m. Lit. 'Running water'; a very fine kind of muslin. — āb-rez, s.m. 'Pouring or sprinkling water'; a watering-pot; a shower-bath. — āb-i-zar, s.m. Water of gold; white wine. — āb-i-zulāl, s.m. Pure, clear, cool water; an infusion. — āb-shār, s.m.f. Water-fall, cascade. — āb-shinās, s.m. One who takes soundings in a ship, lead-man, pilot. — āb-i-shor, s.m. Salt water; sea-water; the sea. — āb-shora, s.m. Water cooled with saltpetre; a sherbet made of lime-juice and sugar (generally cooled), lemonade. — āb-i-ʻashrat, s.m. Wine; semen genitale. — āb-kār, s.m. A water-carrier; sprinkling (rare); a distiller or seller of spirituous liquors; a drinker of spirituous liquors. — āb-kārī, s.f. The business of a distiller; distillery; liquor-shop, tavern; duty on the manufacture and sale of spirituous liquors, excise. — āb-kash, s.m. A drawer of water

s.f. The act of drawing or carrying water. — āb-i-kaus̤ar, s.m. The water of the river Kausar, fabled to flow in Paradise with milk and nectar; nectar. — āb-guẕār, s.m. Passage of a river, ford, ferry; an expeditious messenger, courier; an express. — āb-guẕar, s.m. Water-course, channel for water, canal. — āb-i-gosht, s.m. = āb-josh, q.v. — āb-i-gohar, s.m. Lit. 'Water of pearls'; pure, limpid water. — āb-gīr, s.m. Receptacle for water, reservoir, pond, ditch; a weaver's brush. — āb-gīna, s.m. Lit. 'Possessed of lustre or clearness'; mirror, looking-glass; drinking-glass; bottle; — wine; diamond. — āb-i-manī, s.m. Sperma genitale ( = manī). — āb-i-nuqra, s.m. Silver reduced to a liquid state; quicksilver. — āb-nai or āb-naë, s.f. The perpendicular tube of a huqqa on which the ćilam is fixed; (in Geography) a strait. — āb-noshī, s.m. Drinking water, (e.g. ćāh-i-āb-noshī). — āb o tāb = āb-tāb, q.v. — āb o dāna = āb-dāna, q.v. — āb o rang, s.m. Lit. 'Water and colour'; beauty; splendour; polish, gloss; joy, pleasure, bliss. — āb o namak, s.m. Lit. 'Water and salt'; sufficiency of water and salt (in a dish); seasoning, condiments; ingredients; savouriness, relish, flavour, taste; — maintenance, support. āb o hawā, s.f. Lit. 'Water and air' (of a place); air, climate. — āb-yār, s.m. Waterer, sprinkler
s.f. Watering, sprinkling; — a kind of thin linen cloth; a species of dove. — āb-i-yaḵẖnī, s.m. Water in which meat has been boiled down, broth; gravy ( = āb-i-gosht).

ety: prob. formed by analogy after jab, tab, &c. q.v.
def: adv. Now, presently, just now, now-a-days, a little while ago, recently. — ab-bhī, adv. Even now, yet, as yet, still. — ab-tab, adv. Now and then, presently, very shortly
subs: To put off continually, to delay; to evade, shuffle

To be in a dying state, to be at the point of death. — ab-tak and ab-talak, adv. Up to the present time, till now; as yet, hitherto; all this time. — ab-taʼīṅ, ab-torī, ab-toṛī, ab-teṅ = ab-tak, q.v. — ab-se, adv. From this time, henceforth, henceforward, in future, hence

Far be it from us! Heaven preserve us! Avaunt! (The phrase is generally used — especially by women — to deprecate the recurrence of a past misfortune). — ab-kā, adj. (f. -kī). Of the present; for the present; this, the present; the next, the following, the coming; new, fresh
adv. This time, the next time

Idem. — ab-lag, ab-loṅ, ab-le, ab-leṅ, adv. = ab-tak, q.v. — ab-na-tab, adv. Neither now nor then; neither here (on earth) nor in the world to come; nowhere; never. — ab-hī, adv. Just now, this very moment, instantly, immediately; already.
wety: Formed by analogy after جب (jab) and تب (tab).

ety: v.n. fr. عبي, or عبأ 'to prepare,' &c.
def: s.f. A woollen cloak or habit, very loose, and open in front all the way down the centre (not unlike a boat-cloak; it is usually of a black colour with stripes, and is worn by dervises or faqīrs).
s.m. Fathers; forefathers, ancestors; family, race; lineage, line.
s.m. Adverse fortune; misfortune, ill-luck.
s.m. A swallow.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ابابیل (abābīl), from Arabic أَبَابِيل (ʔabābīl).

def: s.m. That which has not escaped.

adj. Inhabited, populated, peopled; full of buildings and inhabitants, populous; settled (as a colony or town); cultivated; stored; full; occupied; — city, town (in this sense it commonly occurs as the last member of compounds, e.g. Faiẓābād, Ilāhābād); — flourishing, prosperous; pleasant; happy. — ābād-bashī, s.f. First assessment of newly settled or cultivated land. — ābād-rahnā, To be full, stored, flourishing, prosperous, happy or comfortable. — ābād-kār, s.m. The first settler on waste land. — ābād-karnā, v.t. To make (a place) habitable; to people, settle, found (a colony, &c.); to build and plant; to cultivate; to make (the heart, dil) rejoice, to comfort, rejoice (the heart); to build up (a house or family, ghar), beget (children). — ābād honā, To be full, flourishing, &c. ( = ābād rahnā, q.v.); to be occupied (as a house); to have the prospect of issue; to be married.
s.m. Endless time, prospective eternity (opp. to azal, q.v.); eternity. — abad-tak, adv. To all eternity.
s.m. (pl. of بعد), Distances; divarications; dimensions. — Abʻād-i-s̤alās̤a, s.m. The three dimensions (of a solid body).
adj. (comp. & super. of بعيد), More, most, or very distant or remote; further; furthest.

adj. = آباد ābād, q.v. adj. Inhabited, populated, peopled; full of buildings and inhabitants, populous; settled (as a colony or town); cultivated; stored; full; occupied; — city, town (in this sense it commonly occurs as the last member of compounds, e.g. Faiẓābād, Ilāhābād); — flourishing, prosperous; pleasant; happy. — ābād-bashī, s.f. First assessment of newly settled or cultivated land. — ābād-rahnā, To be full, stored, flourishing, prosperous, happy or comfortable. — ābād-kār, s.m. The first settler on waste land. — ābād-karnā, v.t. To make (a place) habitable; to people, settle, found (a colony, &c.); to build and plant; to cultivate; to make (the heart, dil) rejoice, to comfort, rejoice (the heart); to build up (a house or family, ghar), beget (children). — ābād honā, To be full, flourishing, &c. ( = ābād rahnā, q.v.); to be occupied (as a house); to have the prospect of issue; to be married.
adv. Eternally, for ever; never.
s.f. A habitation; a cultivated, populous, or pleasant place; population; cultivation; abundance; prosperity; advancement; civilization; enjoyment, pleasure.
For all compounds having ābād for the first member, v.s.v. آباد ābād.
adj. (f. -ā), = ابدهي abadhya, q.v. s.m. A postulate.
adj. (f. -ī), Unobstructed, unrestrained, unfettered; unoppressed; true; without difficulty; not obstructing, not opposing.
s.f. Segment of the base of a triangle.
s.m. A postulate.
adj. (f. -ā), Not deserving death, not to be slain, inviolable, sacred.

s.f. Inhabited spot or place; colony; population, number of inhabitants; cultivated place; cultivation; the part of a village lands brought under cultivation; increased assessment ( = beshī); prosperity; state of comfort; happiness, joy, pleasure.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آبادی (ābādi). Equivalent to آباد (ābād) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. Eternal, without end, everlasting, immortal.
adj. Belonging to or descending from one's ancestors, paternal.
adj. & adv. Dumb, speechless.
adv. Downwards; below, beneath; southwards.
adj. & s.m. Without strength or power, weak, feeble, infirm, impotent, frail, enervated
adj. (fem. of the preceding), Weak, delicate, &c.: — s.f. A woman; a delicate woman; a beautiful woman.
s.f. Weakness, debility, frailty.
s.m. Weakness, frailty, &c.
s.m. Weakness, frailty, &c.
s.f. = S ابلتو अबलत्व abalatwa, s.m. Weakness, &c. (see abalāpan).
s.m. = S ابلتا अबलता abalatā, s.f. Weakness, &c. (see abalāpan).
s.f. A row, range; a continuous line.
adj. (f. -inī), = ابل abal, q.v. adj. & s.m. Without strength or power, weak, feeble, infirm, impotent, frail, enervated
s.m. = اولوکن avalokan, q.v. s.m. Looking at, beholding; sight, view, inspection.
s.m. The eighth month of the Persian year.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آبان (ābān), related to آب (āb, “water”).

def: s.m. This side, the near bank of a river.
adv. Now, at present ( = اب ab); — adj. Weak, &c. ( = ابل abal).
adj. & adv. Clumsy; coarse, rough: out of all time and tune, jarring, discordant, inharmonious, confused (music).
s.m. = آبهرن ābharan, q.v. s.m. Jewels, ornaments; decoration, finery. (The popular form is abran.)
= اورشن avarshan, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Unsubmissive to another's will, disobedient; unrestrained; unsubdued; not under control: not having one's own free will; in subjection to another; helpless, powerless; without choice.

ety: inf. n. of عبث 'to play, sport'
def: s.m. Play, sport, trifling, frivolity, vanity, absurdity; — adj. & adv. Trifling, frivolous; vain, idle, absurd, nugatory, profitless, bootless; — in vain, uselessly, bootlessly, idly, absurdly
adj. (f. -ā), Without clothes, naked.
s.m. Father; superior; — a consummate knave. — abbā-jān, abbā-jānī, dear father.
(intens. n. fr. عبس 'to look stern,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'Having a stern or morose countenance'; a proper name; — the plant Marvel of Peru, Mirabilis jalapa.
adj. Of the colour of the flower of the Marvel of Peru; red; — s.f. The flower of the Marvel of Peru ( = gul-ě-ʻabbās).
For compounds having ab for the first member v.s.v. اب ab.
s.f. = اپسرا apsarā, q.v. s.f. A female dancer in the court of Indra; a nymph; a courtesan. — apsarā-swar, s.m. The notes or echoes of the apsarās.

ety: v.n. fr. عبد 'to serve'; see ʻibādat
def: s.m. A slave, bondsman; a servant (of God), a worshipper; a devotee
subs: Servant of God; a proper name.

def: s.m. Cloud; year, æra.

(pl. of بدل badal, or بديل badīl), m. Lit. 'The substitutes or lieutenants'; — 1˚. Certain righteous persons of whom the world is never destitute, and by whom God rules the earth: 2˚. The substitutes and successors of the prophets: (hence,) 3˚. A religious person, hermit, devotee, saint, enthusiast: 4˚. A religious fraternity; a class of wandering Mohammadan saints.
adj. Of or relating to an abdāl or devotee.
s.f. Segment of the base of a triangle.
s.m. The ocean. — abdhi-kaph, s.m. Cuttle-fish bone.
s.m. One who has separated himself from worldly feeling and obligation; a Hindū faqir, a jogī (q.v.); a devotee; (commonly) a worshipper of the god Śiva.
s.f. The being a slave, the state or condition of a slave, servitude, slavery.
interj. Holloa! ho there! you fellow! Sirrah! you rascal! — abe-tabe-se pesh ānā, v.n. To address (one) with undue familiarity, or disrespectfully or rudely; to treat (one) ceremoniously
s.f. Lateness, delay
subs: after time; late in the season; unseasonably.

def: v.s.v. ابے abe.
s.f. Light, splendour, beauty.
s.m. Unfortunateness; misfortune, calamity, ill-luck.
(f. -ī), = ابهاگی abhāgī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Having ill-luck, unfortunate, unlucky, ill-starred; destitute: s.m. An unfortunate or unlucky wight.
s.m. Unfortunateness, ill-fate, lucklessness.
adj. (f. -inī), Having ill-luck, unfortunate, unlucky, ill-starred; destitute: s.m. An unfortunate or unlucky wight.
s.m. = ابهاگ abhāg; — adj. (f. -ā), = ابهاگی abhāgī, q.v. s.m. Unfortunateness; misfortune, calamity, ill-luck.
adj. (f. -matī), = ابهاگي abhāgī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Having ill-luck, unfortunate, unlucky, ill-starred; destitute: s.m. An unfortunate or unlucky wight.
part. adj. Not to be eaten; unlawful to be eaten: — s.m. Unlawful or forbidden food.
adj. (f. -ā), Unbelieving, undevout; unattached, indifferent. — s.m. An unbeliever, &c.
s.f. Want of devotion to; want of faith; unbelief, impiety; disregard; want of desire; indifference.
adj. & s.m. (f. -matī), Undevoted to; unbelieving; indifferent to: — An unbeliever.
s.m. A cloud. — abhāl-ānā and abhāl ćhānā, v.n. To be clouded or overcast.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ابھال (abhal) (c. 1592) / آبھال (ābhal) (c. 1638), from Sanskrit *अभ्रतल (*abhratala, “sky”) (अभ्र (abhrá) + तल (tála)) or Sanskrit *अभ्रकाल (*abhrakāla, “cloudy weather”). Cognate with Marathi आभाळ (ābhāḷ).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Unbefitting, unbecoming, unsuitable: distasteful; unacceptable.
adj. Not breakable; strong; indestructible, perishable; not broken.
s.m. Non-existence; absence; extinction, annihilation; poverty, want, destitution
adj. (f. -ā), Light, of little weight.
s.m.(?) The narcissus; jasmine; anemone.
adj. (f. -ā), Without credit, respect, or character. — abharam karnā, v.t. To dishonour, disgrace.
s.m. Jewels, ornaments; decoration, finery. (The popular form is abran.)
s.m. Jewels, ornaments; decoration, finery. (The popular form is abran.)
adj. Of or relating to the narcissus, &c.; — s.f. = ʻabhar.
s.m. Appearance, semblance, likeness; semblance of a reason; fancy; thought; impression, idea; intention, purpose.
s.m. A little; a few.
s.m. Introduction, preface, preamble, exordium
s.m. Introduction, preface, preamble, exordium
s.m. (f. abhāvatā), Non-existence; deficiency; want.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Without fear, fearless, undaunted, bold, courageous; — a fearless person, &c.; — the root of a fragrant grass, Andropogon muricatum. — abhay-patra, s.m. Assurance of safety or protection, a safe-conduct; encouragement; amnesty
ex: Come here right now/at once/this instant.

def: s.f. A courageous female ( = abhaya, q.v.); — adj. Unbetitting, &c. ( = abhānā, q.v.)
s.f. A courageous woman; — the plant Terminalia citrina.
s.m. = abhay-patra, q.v. s.v. ابهي abhay. s.m.: v.s.v. ابهي abhay.
s.m.: v.s.v. ابهي abhay.
(or abhay-bāk) = abhay-patra, q.v. s.v. ابهي abhay. s.m.: v.s.v. ابهي abhay.
s.m. Absence of difference or distinction; identity; similarity
subs: (f. -ā), similar; identical, of one kind; united, fastened together; known, celebrated.

def: adj. Not to be divided or broken, indivisible, impenetrable, inseparable.
= ابهيد abhed, q.v. s.m. Absence of difference or distinction; identity; similarity
subs: (f. -ā), similar; identical, of one kind; united, fastened together; known, celebrated.

def: (i.e. अब ab+emphat. aff. ही hī), v.s.v. اب ab.
ex: Come here right now/at once/this instant.

def: An inseparable Sanskrit prefix (the reverse of अनु anu), denoting priority in time or place; before; against; with respect to.
= ابهواد abhirād, q.v.
= ابهوادن abhivādan, q.v. s.m. Respectful salutation, a bow or prostration to a person (generally including the mention of his name or title); kissing the feet in adoration.
part. adj. Defeated, overcome, subdued, subjected, domineered over.
(local) = ابهي abhī, q.v. An inseparable Sanskrit prefix (the reverse of अनु anu), denoting priority in time or place; before; against; with respect to.
s.m. Incantation; employment of spells for a malevolent purpose; a charm or spell causing death or disease.
s.m. A vocabulary, a dictionary.
s.m. Approaching, coming forward.
adj. Noble, well-born; inbred, innate; fit, proper; wise, learned.
s.m. The 21st nakshatra, or lunar asterism; the 8th muhūrt, or division of the day.
adj. (f. -ā), Knowing, clever, skilful, conversant with: — s.m. A patron.
s.m. Assault, attack; undertaking, enterprise.
= ابهلاش abhilāsh, q.v. s.m. = S ابهلاشا अभिलाषा abhilāshā (vulg.abhilākhā), s.f. Desire, wish, longing, strong inclination, craving; affection, love, passion.
= ابهلاشا abhilāshā, q.v. s.f. = S ابهلاش अभिलाष abhilāsh (vulg.abhilākh), s.m. Desire, wish, longing, strong inclination, craving; affection, love, passion.
s.m. = S ابهلاشا अभिलाषा abhilāshā (vulg.abhilākhā), s.f. Desire, wish, longing, strong inclination, craving; affection, love, passion.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अभिलाषा (abhilāṣā). Doublet of اَبِھلاکھا (abhilākhā).

def: s.f. = S ابهلاش अभिलाष abhilāsh (vulg.abhilākh), s.m. Desire, wish, longing, strong inclination, craving; affection, love, passion.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Wishing, desiring; longing for; covetous, greedy
part. adj. Desired, wished.

s.m. High opinion of self, self-conceit, pride, haughtiness. — abhimān-putra, s.m. (f. -kā or -ikā), A foster-son. — abhimān karnā, To behave proudly or conceitedly; to be proud or conceited. — abhimān galānā, (-kā), To bring down (one's) pride, to humble; to get rid of (one's own) pride.
s.f. Pride, arrogance, &c. ( = abhimān).
adj. (f. -inī). = S ابهمانيه अभिमान्य abhimānya, adj.(f. -ā). Proud, conceited, haughty, arrogant
adj. (f. -ā). = S ابهماني अभिमानी abhimānī, adj.(f. -inī). Proud, conceited, haughty, arrogant
adj. = ابهمانيه abhimānya, q.v. adj. (f. -ā). = S ابهماني अभिमानी abhimānī, adj.(f. -inī). Proud, conceited, haughty, arrogant
part. Admitted, assented to, consented to, accepted; approved of, chosen; wished, desired.
s.f. Agreeableness; desireableness; desire; love.
adj. (f.-ā), Turning towards, facing, in front; before, present
s.f. Presence, proximity.
s.m. Praise, applause, approbation; consent.
adj. (f. -ā), Recent, new, quite new.
adj. Undivided, unseparated; one — s.m. An integer, a whole number.
s.m. Aim, purpose, intention, design, wish, desire; sentiment; goal; the main purport of a book, &c.
part. adj. Intended, designed.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Desired, wished for.
s.m. A cowherd. (See اهير ahīr.)
adj. (f. -ā), Agreeable, pleasing, delightful; beautiful, charming.
s.f. Desire, delight, pleasure, taste, relish. — abhiruć rakhnā (-meṅ). To have or take delight (in).
s.m. Meeting, rendezvous, tryst, a lover's appointment; attack, assault.
adj. (f. -inī), Going to meet; keeping an assignation: — s.m. A party to an assignation.
s.f. A woman who goes to meet her lover or keeps an assignation; a lewd woman.

s.m. Sprinkling; inaugurating or consecrating by sprinkling water (usually that of the Ganges or other sacred river); inauguration of a king, royal unction; installation; religious bathing, ablution; bathing of the idol to which worship is offered; — a religious ceremony consisting of the presentation of a variety of articles (such as fruits, gems, &c.) along with water or with fluid substances for bathing the idol to which worship is offered.
part. adj. Sprinkled, baptized; anointed to an office, inaugurated, enthroned; imbued with.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Desired, beloved, cherished.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Not terrified; fearless; undaunted, unalarmed
s.f. Fearlessness, intrepidity.
s.m. Reverential salutation; — unfriendly or opprobrious language, abuse.
s.m. Respectful salutation, a bow or prostration to a person (generally including the mention of his name or title); kissing the feet in adoration.
s.m. Curve; crookedness; roundness; extension; fullness; a vault; the expanded hood of the cobra capella; — enjoyment; satiety, surfeit.
adj. Unfit for use; — unpossessed, unenjoyed.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī). Not partaking in; not possessing or enjoying; — a non-participator.
adj. = ابهوجيه abhojya, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Not to be eaten, prohibited as food; impure: — forbidden food.
s.m. Not eating, fasting, abstinence.
adj. & sm. (f. -inī), Not having eaten; fasting: — a faster.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Not to be eaten, prohibited as food; impure: — forbidden food.
s.m. The sky; a cloud ( = ابر abr).
s.m. Talc; mica ( = ابرك abrak).
adj. Made or consisting of talc.
= ابهي abhī, q.v. s.v. اب ab. An inseparable Sanskrit prefix (the reverse of अनु anu), denoting priority in time or place; before; against; with respect to.
s.m. Ornaments, jewels, jewellery, finery.
= ابهو abhū = ابهي abhī, q.v. = ابهي abhī, q.v. s.v. اب ab. An inseparable Sanskrit prefix (the reverse of अनु anu), denoting priority in time or place; before; against; with respect to.
s.m. Ornaments, jewels, jewellery, finery.
part. adj. Non-existent; not past. — abhūt-ripu, adj. Without enemies.
part. (f. -ā), Arrived
subs: a visitor; a devotee, a faqīr.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Interior, internal, inward (see bhītarī); — s.m. inner part, interior, inside, middle, included space (see bhītar).
adj. Interior, internal.
s.m. Study; meditation; practice, exercise; the frequent repetition of a thing in order to fix it on the memory; repetition; intimate acquaintance (with), familiarity.
adj. (f. -inī), Studious; practising; repeating; exercising: — s.m. A student.
part. adj. Practised. exercised; learnt by heart; repeated; studied; accustomed, habituated; experienced.
s.m. Rising as a mark of respect; rising; elevation; dignity; rise, origin.
adj. Of or belonging to water, aquatic; watery, moist, humid; irrigated; watery blue, light blue
subs: s.f. light blue colour; — a kind of bread
(made of water in place of milk and butter). — ābī g̠ẖoṛā, s.m. The sea-horse, river horse.

def: = ابهي abhī, q.v. An inseparable Sanskrit prefix (the reverse of अनु anu), denoting priority in time or place; before; against; with respect to.
= اووادي avivādī, q.v. adj. Not contentious or disputatious, peaceable, conciliatory, gentle; — s.m. A peaceable person, &c.
For compounds having āb for the first member v.s.v. آب āb.
= اوويك avivek, q.v. adj. Without judgment or discrimination ( = avivekī, q.v.); — s.m. Absence of discrimination or judgment, indiscretion, ignorance, stupidity.
= اوويکتا avivekatā, q.v. s.f. Want of judgment, inconsiderateness, imprudence, &c. ( = avivek).
= اوويکي avivekī, q.v. adj. Undiscriminating, having no judgment, indiscreet, ignorant, superficial, short-sighted; — s.m. An undiscriminating person, &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Motionless, unmoved, unshaken, resolute, firm.
s.m. Want of consideration, thoughtlessness; want of judgment or discrimination; injustice.
adj. Destitute of consideration or reflection, inconsiderate, unreflecting, thoughtless; unjust
subs: (f. -inī) an unjust man; an ill-advised person.

def: adj. = اوچهين avićhīn, q.v. adj. Without interval, close, dense, coarse, uninterrupted.

ety: act. part. of عبد 'to serve, worship,' &c.
def: s.m. A server, worshipper, adorer (of God), a votary, a devotee; a recluse.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عابد ('ābid), itself from Arabic عَابِد (ʕābid).

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻabd), Servants; worshippers.
adj. & adv. Void of, or contrary to, rule; irregular: irregularly.
s.f. = اوديا avidyā, q.v. s.f. Ignorance, stupidity
subs: adj. Unlearned, illiterate, ignorant; — s.m. An illiterate person.

def: s.m. Bad grain; grain that does not germinate.
adj. (f. -ā), Unapparent, imperceptible; hidden; undetermined, unknown; unintelligible. — abikt-ganit, s.f. The arithmetic of unknown quantities, algebra.
s.m. A blister, a pustule (of small pox, &c.). — ābla-i-farang, s.m. The European pox, syphilis; bubo.
= اولمب avilamb, q.v. adj. Not delaying, prompt, quick, ready, expeditious; — s.m. Haste, despatch, quickness, diligence.
s.m. Exemption from destruction, loss, or decay; safety.
adj. (f. -inī), Indestructible, imperishable, exempt from decay or loss; everlasting, immortal; safe; entire
s.m. Want of good manners; indecorum; want of modesty; rude behaviour, rudeness, pertness; wantonness.
adj. Without point or dot.
adj. (f. -ā), Badly trained; ill-mannered; misbehaving, acting ill or improperly; unchaste, unfaithful (conjugally); wanton; pert, petulant: — s.m. An unfaithful spouse.
s.f. An immodest, unchaste, or unfaithful woman or wife.
s.m. Red powder used at the holī festival.
part. adj. & s.m. Crossing, passing; — a passenger.
s.m. Saffron; ambergris; a mixture of perfumes compounded with saffron; a kind of powder used at the saturnalia of the holī (i.q. abīr).
adj. Of saffron, &c.; resembling saffron, &c.
s.m. = اورودهہ avirodh, q.v.
adj. = اورودهي avirodhī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Not contentious or combative, peaceable, tranquil, quiet: — a peaceable person.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Without desire, indifferent; without application

= ابهشيك abhishek, q.v. s.m. Sprinkling; inaugurating or consecrating by sprinkling water (usually that of the Ganges or other sacred river); inauguration of a king, royal unction; installation; religious bathing, ablution; bathing of the idol to which worship is offered; — a religious ceremony consisting of the presentation of a variety of articles (such as fruits, gems, &c.) along with water or with fluid substances for bathing the idol to which worship is offered.
= آبسته ābista, q.v. adj. Pregnant (a woman); with young (an animal); new-born (child).
adj. Pregnant (a woman); with young (an animal); new-born (child).
s.f. Pregnancy; the state of being with young.
s.f. Pregnancy; the state of being with young.
s.m. Want of confidence, distrust, disbelief; mistrust, suspicion.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī). Destitute of, or deficient in, belief; incredulous, distrustful; mistrustful, suspicious
s.m. Distrustfulness, suspiciousness.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Unmarried, single
subs: a bachelor. — abiwāhitā, s.f. An unmarried woman; a mistress.

def: s.m. The nymphæa or lotus; the moon; a conch; the tree Barringtonia acutangula; Dhanvantari, the physician of the gods; a million millions.

s.m. 1˚. The first of a series of eight words comprising the letters of the Arabic alphabet in the order in which they were originally disposed (agreeing with that of the Hebrew and Aramaic). 2˚. The arrangement of the Arabic alphabet according to the occurrence of the letters in the said eight words. 3˚. The numerical value of the letters in this arrangement of them. 4˚. The alphabet. — abjad-ḵẖẉān, s.m. One who is learning the alphabet; a beginner (syn. nau-āmoz).
s.m. Rendering permanent; confirmation; establishment; perpetuation; prolongation; preservation.
adj. (f. -inī) & s.m. Without fruit; unfruitful, barren: — a tree without fruit.
adj. (comp. and super. of بخيل baḵẖīl), More, most or very covetous or avaricious.
s.m. Vapours, exhalations, steam, fumes; — raving, delirium.
adj. Simple, silly, foolish; ignorant; bashful. — ablah-fareb, s.m. A sly, wily knave; an impostor.
s.f. Silliness, foolishness, folly.
adj. Black and white, party-coloured, piebald; spotted; pepper-and-salt; grey.
wety: From Arabic أَبْلَق (ʔablaq), itself from Persian ابلک.

def: s.m. A bird of the maina kind; a starling.
s.m. White sugar-candy.

ety: pl. of ابن ibn; — used in comp. only
def: s.m. Sons; people; tribes. — abnā-i-jins, s.m. Those of the same rank, quality, kind, or species; companions, comrades, equals. abnā-i-dahr, s.m. and abnā-i-rozgār, s.m. Sons of the time, contemporaries.
s.f. The earth, world.
s.m. Ebony. — ābnūs-kā kundā, adj. (Lit. 'a log of ebony'); black and ill-featured, repulsive: — s.m. A repulsive man; a blackamoor.
adj. Made of obony; black as ebony; black.
s.m. Ignorance, stupidity
subs: (f. -ā), Unintelligent, stupid, ignorant; puzzled, perplexed. — abodh-gamya, adj. (f. -ā), Incomprehensible.

def: adj. (f. -ā), not to be understood, unintelligible, incomprehensible.
adj. = ابدهہ abudh and ابودهہ abodh, q.v. adj. Deficient in, or void of, understanding or sense, unwise, ignorant, stupid, foolish: — s.m. A fool.

ety: अ + rt. of बोलना bolnā, q.v.
def: adj. (f. -ā), Speechless; silent, mute; dumb.
adj. (f. -ī), Disposed or accustomed to be silent, &c. ( = abol); taciturn; reserved; conceited, vain
subs: s.m. Dumb creature; one incapable of speaking distinctly

ety: Old P. awar, Pehl. abar, Zend awra, S. अभ्र
def: s.m. A cloud; the marks on the paper called abrī (q.v.). — abr-i-āẕur, s.m. A cloud without rain. — abr ćhānā, v.n. Clouds to overshadow; to be cloudy or overcast; to lower. — abr-i-ʻālamgīr, s.m. Rain clouds which cover a large extent of country. — abr-i-qibla, s.m. Clouds which come from the direction of the qibla; clouds heavily charged with rain. — abr khulnā, v.n. Clouds to disperse; (the sky) to be clear. — abr-i-murda, s.m. A sponge.
s.m. The outer fold of a double garment (opposed to استر astar, 'the lining'). — abrā ćaṛhānā (-par). To sow or quilt another, or outer, fold (upon a garment).
s.m. = ابرا abrā, q.v. s.m. The outer fold of a double garment (opposed to استر astar, 'the lining'). — abrā ćaṛhānā (-par). To sow or quilt another, or outer, fold (upon a garment).
adj. (comp. & super. of برد), More, most, or very cold.
= the next Arabicized.
s.m. Talc; mica.
s.m. = آبهرن ābharan, q.v. s.m. Jewels, ornaments; decoration, finery. (The popular form is abran.)
s.m. Bracelet; ankle ornament of gold or silver (cf. abran).
s.m. Bracelet; ankle ornament of gold or silver (cf. abran).
s.m. Just, holy, pious men; saints: dutiful (to parents).
adj. Leprous.
adj. Marked with small specks, speckled: mottled; dapple grey (horse).

ety: for A. عبرة, inf. n. of un. fr. عبر 'to shed tears'
def: s.f. Weeping; grief.
s.m. Raw silk, silk; sewing silk.
adj. Silken, made of silk.
adj. Clouded; mottled; variegated
subs: (also abrī kāg̠aẕ), a kind of thick and shining paper, clouded (obtained from Kashmīr); variegated, or marble paper.

ety: Pehl. brū, Z. brvat, S. भ्रु
def: s.f. Eye-brow. — Abrū-par girī mārnī, To knit the brow, to frown.
(i.e. āb-i-rū), v.s.v. آب āb.
s.m. A vineyard; a garden.
s.m. (pl. of بصر), Views, looks; perception, understanding.
adj. (comp. & super. of باطل bāt̤il), More, most, or very vain or fruitless, &c.

ety: from بتر 'to cut off,' 'to cut off a tail'
def: adj. Ruined, spoiled; deteriorated, vitiated; worthless, destitute of good qualities, dissolute, wanton, disorderly; destitute, miserable, poor; in disorder, disarranged; defective, imperfect; false (as a deal at cards). — abtar karnā, v.t. To ruin, spoil, mar, vitiate, deprave, corrupt; to derange, disarrange, disorder, to shuffle (a pack of cards).
s.f. Deterioration, ruin, decay; worthlessness, wretchedness, poverty; disorder, confusion; disorganization; mismanagement.

ety: used, like ibn, in comb. with a following subst. in the gen., the final ū being shortened in pron. if the Ar. art. precede the subst.
def: s.m. Father; originator, author, &c. — abuʼl-bashar, Lit. 'The father of the human race'; Adam. — abuʼl-ḥāris̤, s.m. The lion (he being among beasts of prey, the strongest to acquire). — abū-bakr, Lit. 'The father of the virgin'; the name of the father-in-law of, and first successor to, Mohammad. — abū-turāb, Lit. 'Father dusty;' a nickname of 'Alī, the son-in-law of Mohammad. — abū-jahl, Lit. 'Father of ignorance,' the name of an uncle of Mohammad, noted for his stubbornness. — abū-ʻalī-sīnā, The name of a celebrated sage and physician. — abū-huraira, Lit. 'Father (owner) of the little cat'; the nickname of one of Mohammad's disciples (who was in the habit of carrying a pet cat about with him).
adj. = abudh, q.v.
subs: s.f. Want of understanding or sense

def: adj. (f. -mati), = abudh and abuddhi (adj.) qq.v. adj. Deficient in, or void of, understanding or sense, unwise, ignorant, stupid, foolish: — s.m. A fool.
adj. Deficient in, or void of, understanding or sense, unwise, ignorant, stupid, foolish: — s.m. A fool.
adj. (f. -ā), Void of, or deficient in, understanding or sense; senseless, stupid.
adj. (f. -ī), Not understood; incomprehensible.

s.m. Doors, gates; chapters, sections; heads or subjects of taxation; sources of public revenue, such as cesses, imposts, assessments on land, &c.: (under Mohammadan rule) taxes imposed in excess of the original assessment.
s.m. = اويو avyava, q.v.
s.f. pl. (of بيت bait), Lit. 'houses'; distichs, verses.
adj. (from بيض), White; more, most, or very splendid or shining.

corr. of اچها aććhā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Good; serviceable; useful; fit; excellent; nice, fine; — sound; hale; healthy; salubrious (as climate); genuine, pure, unadulterated; — pleasant, pleasing, agreeable; amiable, kind benevolent, humane; virtuous, righteous; well-behaved; — clever, skilful (workman); — proper, suitable, fitting; — favourable, propitious; lucky, fortunate; happy; prosperous; — reasonable, moderate, cheap; — fertile, productive (soil)
subs: correctly, properly; nicely, finely; opportunely; — yes, very good, very well, all right; granted; true; quite right
aććhe aććhe, s.m. pl. The good; superiors; great men; men of birth, rank or learning, &c.; — forefathers, ancestors
aććhī t̤araḥ-se, adv. Well, properly, carefully; in a fitting manner, suitably; abundantly; so as one could wish, satisfactorily; adequately
adj. The best of all; the very best
v.t. To do good; to do well or right; — to make (one) well, to restore to health, heal, cure
v.t. To restore to health, heal, cure
v.t. To say yes; to pronounce or call good; to speak well of (-ko)
interj. Well said! how fine! what next!

v.n. To be pleasing, agreeable or palatable, to please; to appear pretty or becoming, to look well; to become, befit, suit; to afford pleasure (to); to derive pleasure or enjoyment (from), to enjoy, relish; to have a good effect, prove beneficial, to benefit
or ho-jānā, v.n. To become or get well, to recover; to be healed or cured; to be in good health.

def: adj. Uneasy, uncomfortable.
adj. (f. -ā), Not possessing or exhibiting the qualities which distinguish men from brutes; without sensation or consciousness; brutish, irrational; senseless.
adj. Brimful, chock-full.
= اچانك aćānak, q.v. adv. Suddenly, unawares, unexpectedly; all at once; by chance; inadvertently; unconsciously; unwittingly
subs: v.t. To take unawares, to surprise.

def: adj. Blind; — s.f. Spectacles.
adj. Not moving; incapable of moving; immovable, firm, fixed, stationary; inanimate; — s.m. A mountain; an aged and infirm person.
s.f. The earth (supposed, as the centre of the universe, to be immovable).
s.m. Rinsing the mouth; the act of sipping a little water from the palm of the hand by way of purification before meals or religious ceremonies
= اچمبا aćambā, q.v. s.m. Wonderful thing, wonder, marvel; wonder, surprise, astonishment, amazement, bewilderment, perplexity
subs: (f. -ī), Wonderful, marvellous, astonishing, surprising; astounding; extraordinary, uncommon, strange, rare
(-se), To marvel (at), be astonished, surprised, astounded, &c. (at)
(or aćambe-)kā, adj. (f. -ī), Surprising, strange, extraordinary, uncommon, new, &c.
(or aćambe-) meṅ ānā, and aćambe honā, To be lost in wonder, astonishment, or amazement; to be astounded, bewildered, perplexed, &c.

def: s.m. Wonderful thing, wonder, marvel; wonder, surprise, astonishment, amazement, bewilderment, perplexity
subs: (f. -ī), Wonderful, marvellous, astonishing, surprising; astounding; extraordinary, uncommon, strange, rare
(-se), To marvel (at), be astonished, surprised, astounded, &c. (at)
(or aćambe-)kā, adj. (f. -ī), Surprising, strange, extraordinary, uncommon, new, &c.
(or aćambe-) meṅ ānā, and aćambe honā, To be lost in wonder, astonishment, or amazement; to be astounded, bewildered, perplexed, &c.

def: s.m. Wonderful thing, wonder, marvel; wonder, surprise, astonishment, amazement, bewilderment, perplexity
subs: (f. -ī), Wonderful, marvellous, astonishing, surprising; astounding; extraordinary, uncommon, strange, rare
(-se), To marvel (at), be astonished, surprised, astounded, &c. (at)
(or aćambe-)kā, adj. (f. -ī), Surprising, strange, extraordinary, uncommon, new, &c.
(or aćambe-) meṅ ānā, and aćambe honā, To be lost in wonder, astonishment, or amazement; to be astounded, bewildered, perplexed, &c.

def: part. adj. Astonished, amazed, astounded; bewildered, perplexed; alarmed.
v.t. To rinse the mouth (before and after eating, and before religious ceremonies, &c.); to sip water; to gargle.
adv. Suddenly, unawares, unexpectedly; all at once; by chance; inadvertently; unconsciously; unwittingly
subs: v.t. To take unawares, to surprise.
ex: وہ اچانک زور سے چلانے لکا۔
vō acānak zor se cillāne lagā.

def: adv. Suddenly, unawares, unexpectedly; all at once; by chance; inadvertently; unconsciously; unwittingly
subs: v.t. To take unawares, to surprise.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Unmoved; immovable, firm, fixed; steady, constant; inactive.
adj. (f. -ā), Not hot-tempered, gentle, tractable.
adj. Immovable, fixed, stationary; inanimate.
s.m. Manner of life, conduct, behaviour; custom, practice, usage; established rule of conduct; institute; religious observance; essential rites and ceremonies
subs: adj. Irregular, out of rule; out of caste; — s.m. An outcast.

def: s.m. Pickles
subs: v.t. To make pickles; to pickle
or kar-denā, v.t. To beat severely, to make (one) smart

def: s.m. = آچاريه āćārya, q.v. s.f. The wife of an āćārya; a spiritual preceptress.
s.m. Manner of life, conduct, deportment; custom, usage, practice; an institute, rite or rule of conduct; religious observance
subs: To behave; to do, act; to practise, make a practice (of, -kā).

def: = آچرن āćaraṇ, q.v. s.m. Manner of life, conduct, deportment; custom, usage, practice; an institute, rite or rule of conduct; religious observance
subs: To behave; to do, act; to practise, make a practice (of, -kā).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Following established rites and practices, observant of religious ceremonies; religious, pious; — one who follows established rules and practices, &c.; a religious man; a devotee.
v.t. To do, act, &c. = āćaraṇ karnā, q.v. s.m. Manner of life, conduct, deportment; custom, usage, practice; an institute, rite or rule of conduct; religious observance
subs: To behave; to do, act; to practise, make a practice (of, -kā).

def: s.m. Guide or instructor in religious matters, especially one who invests the student with the sacrificial thread and instructs him in the Vedas, in the law of sacrifice and the mysteries of religion; priest; founder, or leader of a sect; a learned paṇḍit; a title affixed to the names of learned men.
s.f. The wife of an āćārya; a spiritual preceptress.
s.f. The wife of an āćārya; a spiritual preceptress.
adj. Not cunning, not dexterous or skilful; stupid, inapt; inactive, slow.
s.m. Terrestrial latitude; a die (for playing with); a cube
subs: or aććh-āṅś, s.m. A degree of latitude

def: adj. (f. -ī), Good; serviceable; useful; fit; excellent; nice, fine; — sound; hale; healthy; salubrious (as climate); genuine, pure, unadulterated; — pleasant, pleasing, agreeable; amiable, kind benevolent, humane; virtuous, righteous; well-behaved; — clever, skilful (workman); — proper, suitable, fitting; — favourable, propitious; lucky, fortunate; happy; prosperous; — reasonable, moderate, cheap; — fertile, productive (soil)
subs: correctly, properly; nicely, finely; opportunely; — yes, very good, very well, all right; granted; true; quite right
aććhe aććhe, s.m. pl. The good; superiors; great men; men of birth, rank or learning, &c.; — forefathers, ancestors
aććhī t̤araḥ-se, adv. Well, properly, carefully; in a fitting manner, suitably; abundantly; so as one could wish, satisfactorily; adequately
adj. The best of all; the very best
v.t. To do good; to do well or right; — to make (one) well, to restore to health, heal, cure
v.t. To restore to health, heal, cure
v.t. To say yes; to pronounce or call good; to speak well of (-ko)
interj. Well said! how fine! what next!

v.n. To be pleasing, agreeable or palatable, to please; to appear pretty or becoming, to look well; to become, befit, suit; to afford pleasure (to); to derive pleasure or enjoyment (from), to enjoy, relish; to have a good effect, prove beneficial, to benefit
or ho-jānā, v.n. To become or get well, to recover; to be healed or cured; to be in good health.
ex: اِس سال کیا آپ اچھے تھے؟
is sāl kyā āp acche the?
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀘𑁆𑀙 (accha) without cluster simplification, from Sanskrit अच्छ (accha). Displaced Urdu آچَھا (āchā), from the same source. Cognate with Punjabi اچھا / ਅੱਛਾ (acchā).

def: s.m. Covering; cloak; cloth; garment: — āććhādan karnā. v.t. To cover; conceal.
part. Covered; concealed; guarded.
part. (f. -ā), Covered, concealed; protected.
= اکشر akshar, q.v. s.m. Letter (of the Alphabet); syllable
subs: s.m. A treatise on the alphabet.

def: s.m. An elegant penman or caligrapher; a lettered or scientific person.
s.m. Unbroken or whole grain; whole grains of rice used in religious ceremonies; a few grains of rice; fried grain
subs: s.f. The ceremony of putting a few grains of rice on the forehead of an idol, or of a Brāhman when invited to an entertainment.

def: adj. Imperishable; undecayable
s.m. The eye
subs: s.f. The pupil of the eye.
